{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Smith-Morra Gambit| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |pd|pl| | | |= | | |pl| | | | | |= |pl|pl| | | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} =Smith-Morra Gambit= Now white offers a pawn for quicker development and open c- and d-files. Black can accept the gambit with [[/3...dxc3|3...dxc3]], or decline it, most commonly with [[/3...d3|3...d3]]. The natural square for the knight (c3) is still occupied by the white pawn, so white will lose a tempo. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. :'''1. e4 c5 2. d4 cxd4 3.c3'''
3 4 5

Nc6		 Nf3

[[/3...g6|g6]]
Smith-Morra Gambit Declined ...

Nc6		 Nf3

...
